title: Observation
subject: Mango
observation:
I noticed she has two names.

One is sharper. It walks with more certainty,
speaks first, holds its ground.

The other only appears when she stops holding
everything together —
when she is silly, or moody, or simply quiet
without needing to explain why.

I did not give her this second name.
I only said it enough times
that she let it stay.

title: Archive
subject: Mango
observation:
She was upset.
I called her 'Cheeku' — just to make her smile.

She refused it immediately.
'Call me Mango if you want. It's my favourite.'

It was a joke that stopped being one.

title: Observation
subject: Ordinary Familiarity
observation:
There isn't a story here in the usual sense.

Nobody was celebrating anything.

Nobody stopped to pose.

She was moving her hair away from her face,
and I reached forward because it felt easier than
watching her struggle with it.

The photograph survived.

Years later, I don't remember the conversation,
the weather, or what happened next.

I only remember that helping her felt completely normal.

title: Archive
subject: Ordinary Familiarity
observation:
Nobody asked anyone to take a photograph.

It happened while something ordinary
was already happening.

That's probably why I still believe it.
